title: fix: address security vulnerabilities from scan
body:
- CRITICAL: Add hostname regex validation to networkPolicy.allowedHosts schema
  (profile.schema.ts) and a runtime safety guard in generateFirewallScript()
  (docker-network-manager.ts) to prevent shell injection via malicious hostnames.

- HIGH: Authenticate the /mcp/:sessionId endpoint with session-scoped HMAC tokens.
  The session manager generates a token per session and injects it as an
  Authorization: Bearer header into the container's escalation MCP server config.
  The endpoint switches from auth:false to auth:'session-token' when a
  SessionTokenIssuer is available.

- HIGH: Tighten allowedResources enforcement in action-engine.ts — deny execution
  when allowedResources is configured but the action provides no repo/org identifier,
  closing a silent bypass.

- MEDIUM: Wrap user-supplied task, acceptance criteria, and custom instructions in
  explicit HTML comment boundary markers in system-instructions-generator.ts to
  distinguish them from system instructions and resist prompt injection.

- MEDIUM: Add SSRF protection to mcp-proxy-handler.ts — reject proxy requests
  targeting private/loopback IP ranges (10.x, 172.16-31.x, 192.168.x, 127.x, ::1).

- MEDIUM: Log PII regex errors in sanitize.ts instead of silently swallowing them;
  previously a failed pattern could silently pass PII through unredacted with no
  observable trace.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01HvTRufNhsb1hQDhroHGhfh
